strengths: Demonstrated hands-on experience with agentic workflows and AI-agent infrastructure at re:cinq, including MCP framework contributions.
Strong background in distributed systems, real-time data handling, and microservices from roles at Dunnhumby and Optilyz with high-throughput systems.
Proven leadership in open-source engineering and technical mentoring through GISCollective, workshops, and CI/CD infrastructure development.
concerns: Current career gap since October 2025 raises questions about recent active technical engagement and leadership continuity.
Lack of explicit evidence of scaling engineering teams from 10 to 30+ or building senior technical leadership layers as required.
No clear demonstration of experience in regulated fintech environments, credit risk systems, or compliance infrastructure for financial AI.
response.justification: The candidate brings tangible experience building agentic AI systems and distributed infrastructure, with strong technical craftsmanship and open-source leadership, but lacks demonstrated exposure to fintech regulation, credit risk systems, and scaling engineering teams — critical gaps for this executive role.
